speaker: IAGO
text: Will you think so?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Think so, Iago?

speaker: IAGO
text: What,

speaker: IAGO
text: To kiss in private?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: An unauthorized kiss!

speaker: IAGO
text: Or to be naked with her friend in bed

speaker: IAGO
text: An hour or more, not meaning any harm?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Naked in bed, Iago, and not mean harm?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It is hypocrisy against the devil!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: They that mean virtuously, and yet do so,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The devil their virtue tempts, and they tempt heaven.

speaker: IAGO
text: If they do nothing, ’tis a venial slip.

speaker: IAGO
text: But if I give my wife a handkerchief—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What then?

speaker: IAGO
text: Why then, ’tis hers, my lord, and being hers,

speaker: IAGO
text: She may, I think, bestow ’t on any man.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She is protectress of her honor, too.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: May she give that?

speaker: IAGO
text: Her honor is an essence that’s not seen;

speaker: IAGO
text: They have it very oft that have it not.

speaker: IAGO
text: But for the handkerchief—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: By heaven, I would most gladly have forgot it.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thou saidst—O, it comes o’er my memory

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As doth the raven o’er the infectious house,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Boding to all—he had my handkerchief.

speaker: IAGO
text: Ay, what of that?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That’s not so good now.

speaker: IAGO
text: What

speaker: IAGO
text: If I had said I had seen him do you wrong?

speaker: IAGO
text: Or heard him say (as knaves be such abroad,

speaker: IAGO
text: Who having, by their own importunate suit

speaker: IAGO
text: Or voluntary dotage of some mistress,

speaker: IAGO
text: Convincèd or supplied them, cannot choose

speaker: IAGO
text: But they must blab)—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Hath he said anything?

speaker: IAGO
text: He hath, my lord, but be you well assured,

speaker: IAGO
text: No more than he’ll unswear.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What hath he said?

speaker: IAGO
text: Faith, that he did—I know not what he did.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What? What?

speaker: IAGO
text: Lie—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: With her?

speaker: IAGO
text: With her—on her—what you will.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Lie with her? Lie on her? We say “lie on her”

speaker: OTHELLO
text: when they belie her. Lie with her—Zounds, that’s

speaker: OTHELLO
text: fulsome! Handkerchief—confessions—handkerchief.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To confess and be hanged for his labor.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: First to be hanged and then to confess—I tremble

speaker: OTHELLO
text: at it. Nature would not invest herself in such shadowing

speaker: OTHELLO
text: passion without some instruction. It is not

speaker: OTHELLO
text: words that shakes me thus. Pish! Noses, ears, and

speaker: OTHELLO
text: lips—is ’t possible? Confess—handkerchief—O,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: devil!

speaker: IAGO
text: Work on,

speaker: IAGO
text: My medicine, work! Thus credulous fools are caught,

speaker: IAGO
text: And many worthy and chaste dames even thus,

speaker: IAGO
text: All guiltless, meet reproach.—What ho! My lord!

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord, I say. Othello!

speaker: IAGO
text: How now, Cassio?

speaker: CASSIO
text: What’s the matter?

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord is fall’n into an epilepsy.

speaker: IAGO
text: This is his second fit. He had one yesterday.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Rub him about the temples.

speaker: IAGO
text: No, forbear.

speaker: IAGO
text: The lethargy must have his quiet course.

speaker: IAGO
text: If not, he foams at mouth, and by and by

speaker: IAGO
text: Breaks out to savage madness. Look, he stirs.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do you withdraw yourself a little while.

speaker: IAGO
text: He will recover straight. When he is gone,

speaker: IAGO
text: I would on great occasion speak with you.

speaker: IAGO
text: How is it, general? Have you not hurt your head?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Dost thou mock me?

speaker: IAGO
text: I mock you not, by heaven!

speaker: IAGO
text: Would you would bear your fortune like a man!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A hornèd man’s a monster and a beast.

speaker: IAGO
text: There’s many a beast, then, in a populous city,

speaker: IAGO
text: And many a civil monster.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Did he confess it?

speaker: IAGO
text: Good sir, be a man!

speaker: IAGO
text: Think every bearded fellow that’s but yoked

speaker: IAGO
text: May draw with you. There’s millions now alive

speaker: IAGO
text: That nightly lie in those unproper beds

speaker: IAGO
text: Which they dare swear peculiar. Your case is better.

speaker: IAGO
text: O, ’tis the spite of hell, the fiend’s arch-mock,

speaker: IAGO
text: To lip a wanton in a secure couch

speaker: IAGO
text: And to suppose her chaste! No, let me know,

speaker: IAGO
text: And knowing what I am, I know what she shall be.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, thou art wise, ’tis certain.

speaker: IAGO
text: Stand you awhile apart.

speaker: IAGO
text: Confine yourself but in a patient list.

speaker: IAGO
text: Whilst you were here, o’erwhelmèd with your grief—

speaker: IAGO
text: A passion most unsuiting such a man—

speaker: IAGO
text: Cassio came hither. I shifted him away

speaker: IAGO
text: And laid good ’scuses upon your ecstasy,

speaker: IAGO
text: Bade him anon return and here speak with me,

speaker: IAGO
text: The which he promised. Do but encave yourself,

speaker: IAGO
text: And mark the fleers, the gibes, and notable scorns

speaker: IAGO
text: That dwell in every region of his face.

speaker: IAGO
text: For I will make him tell the tale anew—

speaker: IAGO
text: Where, how, how oft, how long ago, and when

speaker: IAGO
text: He hath and is again to cope your wife.

speaker: IAGO
text: I say but mark his gesture. Marry, patience,

speaker: IAGO
text: Or I shall say you’re all in all in spleen,

speaker: IAGO
text: And nothing of a man.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Dost thou hear, Iago,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I will be found most cunning in my patience,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But (dost thou hear?) most bloody.

speaker: IAGO
text: That’s not amiss.

speaker: IAGO
text: But yet keep time in all. Will you withdraw?

speaker: IAGO
text: Now will I question Cassio of Bianca,

speaker: IAGO
text: A huswife that by selling her desires

speaker: IAGO
text: Buys herself bread and clothes. It is a creature

speaker: IAGO
text: That dotes on Cassio—as ’tis the strumpet’s plague

speaker: IAGO
text: To beguile many and be beguiled by one.

speaker: IAGO
text: He, when he hears of her, cannot restrain

speaker: IAGO
text: From the excess of laughter. Here he comes.

speaker: IAGO
text: As he shall smile, Othello shall go mad,

speaker: IAGO
text: And his unbookish jealousy must construe

speaker: IAGO
text: Poor Cassio’s smiles, gestures, and light behaviors

speaker: IAGO
text: Quite in the wrong.—How do you, lieutenant?

speaker: CASSIO
text: The worser that you give me the addition

speaker: CASSIO
text: Whose want even kills me.

speaker: IAGO
text: Ply Desdemona well, and you are sure on ’t.

speaker: IAGO
text: Now, if this suit lay in Bianca’s power,

speaker: IAGO
text: How quickly should you speed!

speaker: CASSIO
text: Alas, poor caitiff!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Look how he laughs already!

speaker: IAGO
text: I never knew woman love man so.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Alas, poor rogue, I think i’ faith she loves me.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Now he denies it faintly and laughs it out.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do you hear, Cassio?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Now he importunes him

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To tell it o’er. Go to, well said, well said.

speaker: IAGO
text: She gives it out that you shall marry her.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do you intend it?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Ha, ha, ha!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Do you triumph, Roman? Do you triumph?

speaker: CASSIO
text: I marry her? What, a customer? Prithee bear

speaker: CASSIO
text: some charity to my wit! Do not think it so unwholesome.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Ha, ha, ha!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: So, so, so, so. They laugh that wins.

speaker: IAGO
text: Faith, the cry goes that you marry her.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Prithee say true!

speaker: IAGO
text: I am a very villain else.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Have you scored me? Well.

speaker: CASSIO
text: This is the monkey’s own giving out. She is

speaker: CASSIO
text: persuaded I will marry her out of her own love and

speaker: CASSIO
text: flattery, not out of my promise.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Iago beckons me. Now he begins the story.

speaker: CASSIO
text: She was here even now. She haunts me in

speaker: CASSIO
text: every place. I was the other day talking on the

speaker: CASSIO
text: sea-bank with certain Venetians, and thither comes

speaker: CASSIO
text: the bauble. By this hand, she falls thus about my

speaker: CASSIO
text: neck!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Crying, “O dear Cassio,” as it were; his

speaker: OTHELLO
text: gesture imports it.

speaker: CASSIO
text: So hangs and lolls and weeps upon me, so

speaker: CASSIO
text: shakes and pulls me. Ha, ha, ha!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Now he tells how she plucked him to my

speaker: OTHELLO
text: chamber.—O, I see that nose of yours, but not that

speaker: OTHELLO
text: dog I shall throw it to.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Well, I must leave her company.

speaker: IAGO
text: Before me, look where she comes.

speaker: CASSIO
text: ’Tis such another fitchew—marry, a perfumed

speaker: CASSIO
text: one!—What do you mean by this haunting

speaker: CASSIO
text: of me?

speaker: BIANCA
text: Let the devil and his dam haunt you! What did

speaker: BIANCA
text: you mean by that same handkerchief you gave me

speaker: BIANCA
text: even now? I was a fine fool to take it! I must take

speaker: BIANCA
text: out the work? A likely piece of work, that you

speaker: BIANCA
text: should find it in your chamber and know not who

speaker: BIANCA
text: left it there! This is some minx’s token, and I must

speaker: BIANCA
text: take out the work! There, give it your hobbyhorse.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Wheresoever you had it, I’ll take out no work on ’t.

speaker: CASSIO
text: How now, my sweet Bianca? How now? How now?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: By heaven, that should be my handkerchief!

speaker: BIANCA
text: If you’ll come to supper tonight you may. If

speaker: BIANCA
text: you will not, come when you are next prepared

speaker: BIANCA
text: for.

speaker: IAGO
text: After her, after her!

speaker: CASSIO
text: Faith, I must. She’ll rail in the streets else.

speaker: IAGO
text: Will you sup there?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Faith, I intend so.

speaker: IAGO
text: Well, I may chance to see you, for I would very

speaker: IAGO
text: fain speak with you.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Prithee come. Will you?

speaker: IAGO
text: Go to; say no more.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: How shall I murder him,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Iago?

speaker: IAGO
text: Did you perceive how he laughed at his vice?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O Iago!

speaker: IAGO
text: And did you see the handkerchief?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Was that mine?

speaker: IAGO
text: Yours, by this hand! And to see how he prizes

speaker: IAGO
text: the foolish woman your wife! She gave it him, and

speaker: IAGO
text: he hath giv’n it his whore.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I would have him nine years a-killing! A fine

speaker: OTHELLO
text: woman, a fair woman, a sweet woman!

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, you must forget that.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, let her rot and perish and be damned

speaker: OTHELLO
text: tonight, for she shall not live. No, my heart is turned

speaker: OTHELLO
text: to stone. I strike it, and it hurts my hand. O, the

speaker: OTHELLO
text: world hath not a sweeter creature! She might lie by

speaker: OTHELLO
text: an emperor’s side and command him tasks.

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, that’s not your way.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Hang her, I do but say what she is! So

speaker: OTHELLO
text: delicate with her needle, an admirable musician—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, she will sing the savageness out of a bear!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of so high and plenteous wit and invention!

speaker: IAGO
text: She’s the worse for all this.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, a thousand, a thousand times!—And then

speaker: OTHELLO
text: of so gentle a condition!

speaker: IAGO
text: Ay, too gentle.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nay, that’s certain. But yet the pity of it,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Iago! O, Iago, the pity of it, Iago!

speaker: IAGO
text: If you are so fond over her iniquity, give her

speaker: IAGO
text: patent to offend, for if it touch not you, it comes

speaker: IAGO
text: near nobody.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I will chop her into messes! Cuckold me?

speaker: IAGO
text: O, ’tis foul in her.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: With mine officer!

speaker: IAGO
text: That’s fouler.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Get me some poison, Iago, this night. I’ll not

speaker: OTHELLO
text: expostulate with her lest her body and beauty

speaker: OTHELLO
text: unprovide my mind again. This night, Iago.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do it not with poison. Strangle her in her bed,

speaker: IAGO
text: even the bed she hath contaminated.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Good, good. The justice of it pleases. Very

speaker: OTHELLO
text: good.

speaker: IAGO
text: And for Cassio, let me be his undertaker. You

speaker: IAGO
text: shall hear more by midnight.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Excellent good.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What trumpet is that same?

speaker: IAGO
text: I warrant something from Venice.

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Tis Lodovico. This comes from the Duke.

speaker: IAGO
text: See, your wife’s with him.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: God save you, worthy general.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: With all my heart, sir.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: The Duke and the Senators of Venice greet you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I kiss the instrument of their pleasures.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And what’s the news, good cousin Lodovico?

speaker: IAGO
text: I am very glad to see you, signior.

speaker: IAGO
text: Welcome to Cyprus.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: I thank you. How does Lieutenant Cassio?

speaker: IAGO
text: Lives, sir.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Cousin, there’s fall’n between him and my lord

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: An unkind breach, but you shall make all well.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Are you sure of that?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: My lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: “This fail you not to do, as you

speaker: OTHELLO
text: will”—

speaker: LODOVICO
text: He did not call; he’s busy in the paper.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Is there division ’twixt my lord and Cassio?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: A most unhappy one. I would do much

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: T’ atone them, for the love I bear to Cassio.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Fire and brimstone!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: My lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Are you wise?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What, is he angry?

speaker: LODOVICO
text: May be the letter moved him.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: For, as I think, they do command him home,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Deputing Cassio in his government.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: By my troth, I am glad on ’t.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Indeed?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: My lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I am glad to see you mad.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Why, sweet Othello!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Devil!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I have not deserved this.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: My lord, this would not be believed in Venice,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Though I should swear I saw ’t. ’Tis very much.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Make her amends. She weeps.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, devil, devil!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If that the Earth could teem with woman’s tears,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Each drop she falls would prove a crocodile.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Out of my sight!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I will not stay to offend you.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Truly an obedient lady.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: I do beseech your Lordship call her back.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Mistress.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: My lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What would you with her, sir?

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Who, I, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, you did wish that I would make her turn.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Sir, she can turn, and turn, and yet go on,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And turn again. And she can weep, sir, weep.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And she’s obedient, as you say, obedient.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Very obedient.—Proceed you in your tears.—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Concerning this, sir—O, well-painted passion!—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I am commanded home.—Get you away.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll send for you anon.—Sir, I obey the mandate

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And will return to Venice.—Hence, avaunt!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Cassio shall have my place. And, sir, tonight

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I do entreat that we may sup together.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: You are welcome, sir, to Cyprus. Goats and monkeys!

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Is this the noble Moor, whom our full senate

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Call all in all sufficient? Is this the nature

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Whom passion could not shake, whose solid virtue

speaker: LODOVICO
text: The shot of accident nor dart of chance

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Could neither graze nor pierce?

speaker: IAGO
text: He is much changed.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Are his wits safe? Is he not light of brain?

speaker: IAGO
text: He’s that he is. I may not breathe my censure

speaker: IAGO
text: What he might be. If what he might he is not,

speaker: IAGO
text: I would to heaven he were.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: What? Strike his wife?

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Faith, that was not so well. Yet would I knew

speaker: IAGO
text: That stroke would prove the worst.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Is it his use?

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Or did the letters work upon his blood

speaker: LODOVICO
text: And new-create this fault?

speaker: IAGO
text: Alas, alas!

speaker: IAGO
text: It is not honesty in me to speak

speaker: IAGO
text: What I have seen and known. You shall observe him,

speaker: IAGO
text: And his own courses will denote him so

speaker: IAGO
text: That I may save my speech. Do but go after

speaker: IAGO
text: And mark how he continues.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: I am sorry that I am deceived in him.
